summary: Added Stage 3F harness/config/prompt project adapter detection, bounded read-only config and prompt parsing, safe prompt path handling, project seeding integration, focused fixture tests, and durable planning/handoff evidence.

tests_run.command: uv run --no-sync python -B -m pytest tests/test_projects.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
tests_run.summary: 36 passed; covered generic registry behavior, planning SQLite adapter behavior, structured markdown adapter behavior, harness/config adapter selection, read-only prompt validation, project-seed-tasks apply behavior, malformed and oversized harness findings, unsafe Windows/POSIX prompt paths, tasks-array source paths, markerless generic fallback, and target no-mutation checks.
Planning integrity checks passed before and after Stage 3F completion was recorded.
Story Loop status reported Stage 3F as running before completion and queue_state completed with no current AFK task after completion was recorded.
410 passed plus Ruff, format, mypy, CLI smoke checks, file justification, public hygiene, planning integrity, skill inventory, source inventory, protected locks, and uv lock --check.

Adapter selection identifies codex-subag.evidence: HarnessConfigAdapter is selected before GenericRepoAdapter when a bounded harness config includes a codex-subagent-testing marker; markerless harness notes with generic authority markers continue to fall back to generic_repo.

The adapter reads target harness config.evidence: The adapter reads only bounded harness config candidates and repo-local prompt files, caps config and prompt byte sizes plus task entries, rejects unsafe Windows/POSIX prompt paths, reports malformed or oversized inputs through adapter_findings, and tests assert target config/prompt bytes remain unchanged.

Runnable harness/prompt entries become d.evidence: Ready/pending/blocked harness runs or tasks map to harness-config-* ProjectTaskCandidate records with source authority, contract fields, collection-accurate source paths, stable source IDs, and harness verification commands.

project-list and project-seed-tasks JSON.evidence: Existing project-list serialization uses the new adapter facts, and focused tests apply harness adapter candidates into a separate supervisor planning DB while preserving target config text.

Focused tests cover valid harness/config.evidence: tests/test_projects.py now contains thirty-six focused tests covering the named Stage 3F behavior plus existing Stage 3A-E project registry behavior.

Planning SQLite and HANDOFF.md record th.evidence: plans/planning.sqlite3 records the Stage 3F task shape and worker run, and HANDOFF.md records the shaped and completed Stage 3F evidence, checks, risks, and next action.

review.summary: Fresh-thread-style local review found and repaired two actionable issues before completion: unsafe Windows drive/root prompt paths are now rejected, and configs backed by a tasks array now preserve tasks in candidate source_path. No open review findings remain after focused and full verification.

risks: Stage 3 is not fully complete: the tech-resume insights graph adapter remains a future slice.

The harness/config adapter intentionally recognizes one bounded codex-subagent-testing fixture shape; broader harness schema variants may need follow-up support when real fixture evidence is available.
Live Codex Exec launch remains unavailable in this Windows shell because the resolved WindowsApps codex.exe path returns Access is denied.
follow_up_tasks: Shape and implement the tech-resume insights graph adapter.
Expand the harness/config adapter if real codex-subagent-testing schema evidence differs from the public fixture shape.
handoff_notes: Stage 3F adds the third named adapter slice. Continue Stage 3 with the insights graph adapter before Stage 11 MCP.
